---
title: "losgistic_Regression_Excercise"
output: html_document
date: "2026-04-29"
---


```{r}
install.packages(c("tidyverse", "caret"))
```


```{r}
install.packages("patchwork")
```
```{r}
install.packages("yardstick")  
install.packages("ggplot2")
```


```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(caret)
library(patchwork)
library(nnet)       # para softmax (multinom)
library(yardstick)  # para métricas
library(ggplot2)

data(iris)

head(iris, 3)
tail(iris, 3)

iris$es_virginica <- iris$Species == "virginica"
```


```{r}

# MODELO 1: una variable (ancho del pétalo)
X <- iris[, "Petal.Width", drop = FALSE]
y <- iris$es_virginica

set.seed(42)
indices    <- createDataPartition(y, p = 0.75, list = FALSE)
X_train    <- X[indices, , drop = FALSE]
y_train    <- y[indices]
X_train_vec <- X_train[, 1]

train_data <- data.frame(Petal.Width = X_train_vec, es_virginica = y_train)
modelo     <- glm(es_virginica ~ Petal.Width, data = train_data, family = binomial)

X_new    <- seq(0, 3, length.out = 1000)
y_proba  <- predict(modelo, newdata = data.frame(Petal.Width = X_new), type = "response")
decision_boundary <- X_new[which(y_proba >= 0.5)[1]]

plot_data <- data.frame(x = X_new, p_no = 1 - y_proba, p_si = y_proba)
```


```{r}
X2 <- iris[, c("Petal.Length", "Petal.Width")]
y2 <- iris$es_virginica

set.seed(42)
indices2   <- createDataPartition(y2, p = 0.75, list = FALSE)
train_data2 <- data.frame(X2[indices2, ], es_virginica = y2[indices2])
modelo2    <- glm(es_virginica ~ Petal.Length + Petal.Width,
                  data = train_data2, family = binomial)

grilla <- expand.grid(
  Petal.Length = seq(2.9, 7,   length.out = 300),
  Petal.Width  = seq(0.8, 2.7, length.out = 200)
)
grilla$probabilidad <- predict(modelo2, newdata = grilla, type = "response")

coefs      <- coef(modelo2)
x_frontera <- c(2.9, 7)
y_frontera <- -(coefs["Petal.Length"] * x_frontera + coefs["(Intercept)"]) /
               coefs["Petal.Width"]

train_plot <- data.frame(X2[indices2, ], es_virginica = y2[indices2])
```
```{r}
p1 <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = x)) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = 0, ymax = p_no), fill = "#3B82F6", alpha = 0.08) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = 0, ymax = p_si), fill = "#22C55E", alpha = 0.08) +
  geom_line(aes(y = p_no), color = "#3B82F6", linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_line(aes(y = p_si), color = "#22C55E", linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = decision_boundary, color = "#F59E0B",
             linetype = "dotted", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_point(data = data.frame(x = X_train_vec[!y_train], y = 0),
             aes(x = x, y = y), color = "#3B82F6", shape = 15, size = 2, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_point(data = data.frame(x = X_train_vec[y_train], y = 1),
             aes(x = x, y = y), color = "#22C55E", shape = 17, size = 2, alpha = 0.7) +
  annotate("segment", x = decision_boundary, xend = decision_boundary - 0.3,
           y = 0.08, yend = 0.08, color = "#3B82F6",
           arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.25, "cm"))) +
  annotate("segment", x = decision_boundary, xend = decision_boundary + 0.3,
           y = 0.92, yend = 0.92, color = "#22C55E",
           arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.25, "cm"))) +
  annotate("label", x = 0.5, y = 0.75, label = "No virginica",
           color = "#3B82F6", fill = "white", size = 3, label.size = 0) +
  annotate("label", x = 2.5, y = 0.75, label = "Virginica",
           color = "#22C55E", fill = "white", size = 3, label.size = 0) +
  annotate("label", x = decision_boundary, y = 0.5,
           label = paste0("Frontera\n", round(decision_boundary, 2), " cm"),
           color = "#F59E0B", fill = "white", size = 2.8, label.size = 0) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent_format()) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 3), ylim = c(-0.02, 1.02)) +
  labs(title = "Regresión logística — 1 variable",
       subtitle = "Predictor: ancho del pétalo",
       x = "Ancho del pétalo (cm)", y = "Probabilidad") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(plot.title    = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(color = "gray50", size = 9),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank())


p2 <- ggplot() +
  geom_contour_filled(data = grilla,
                      aes(x = Petal.Length, y = Petal.Width, z = probabilidad),
                      alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "RdYlGn", name = "Probabilidad\nvirginica") +
  geom_contour(data = grilla,
               aes(x = Petal.Length, y = Petal.Width, z = probabilidad),
               color = "white", alpha = 0.4, linewidth = 0.4) +
  geom_line(data = data.frame(x = x_frontera, y = y_frontera),
            aes(x = x, y = y), color = "white",
            linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 1.5) +
  geom_point(data = train_plot[!train_plot$es_virginica, ],
             aes(x = Petal.Length, y = Petal.Width),
             color = "#1D4ED8", shape = 15, size = 2.5) +
  geom_point(data = train_plot[train_plot$es_virginica, ],
             aes(x = Petal.Length, y = Petal.Width),
             color = "#15803D", shape = 17, size = 2.5) +
  annotate("label", x = 3.7, y = 1.0, label = "No virginica",
           color = "#1D4ED8", fill = "white", size = 3, label.size = 0) +
  annotate("label", x = 6.3, y = 2.55, label = "Virginica",
           color = "#15803D", fill = "white", size = 3, label.size = 0) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(2.9, 7), ylim = c(0.8, 2.7)) +
  labs(title = "Regresión logística — 2 variables",
       subtitle = "Predictores: longitud y ancho del pétalo",
       x = "Longitud del pétalo (cm)", y = "Ancho del pétalo (cm)") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(plot.title    = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(color = "gray50", size = 9),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "right")


```
```{r}


# DATOS 
data(iris)

X <- iris[, c("Petal.Length", "Petal.Width")]
y <- iris$Species   # 3 clases: setosa, versicolor, virginica

set.seed(0)
indices  <- createDataPartition(y, p = 0.7, list = FALSE)
X_train  <- X[indices, ]
X_test   <- X[-indices, ]
y_train  <- y[indices]
y_test   <- y[-indices]

train_df <- data.frame(X_train, Species = y_train)
test_df  <- data.frame(X_test,  Species = y_test)

# MODELO SOFTMAX

modelo_softmax <- multinom(Species ~ Petal.Length + Petal.Width,
                           data = train_df, trace = FALSE)

# PREDICCIÓN para (5cm longitud, 2cm ancho)
predict(modelo_softmax, 
        newdata = data.frame(Petal.Length = 5, Petal.Width = 2))

predict(modelo_softmax,
        newdata = data.frame(Petal.Length = 5, Petal.Width = 2),
        type = "probs") |> round(3)
```

```{r}
# Predicciones y probabilidades en test set
y_pred  <- predict(modelo_softmax, newdata = test_df)
y_probs <- predict(modelo_softmax, newdata = test_df, type = "probs") |> 
           round(2)

# Tabla de resultados
resultados <- data.frame(
  y_test     = y_test,
  y_pred     = y_pred,
  setosa     = y_probs[, "setosa"],
  versicolor = y_probs[, "versicolor"],
  virginica  = y_probs[, "virginica"]
)

print(resultados)
```
```{r}
# MATRIZ DE CONFUSIÓN
cm <- confusionMatrix(factor(y_pred), factor(y_test))
print(cm)

cm_df <- as.data.frame(table(Predicho = y_pred, Real = y_test))

ggplot(cm_df, aes(x = Predicho, y = Real, fill = Freq)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_text(aes(label = Freq), size = 6, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "#EFF6FF", high = "#1D4ED8") +
  labs(title = "Matriz de Confusión — Iris",
       x = "Valor Predicho", y = "Valor Real") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
        legend.position = "none")
```


```{r}
# MÉTRICAS POR CLASE
cm_tabla <- table(Real = y_test, Predicho = y_pred)

TP <- diag(cm_tabla)
FP <- colSums(cm_tabla) - TP
FN <- rowSums(cm_tabla) - TP
TN <- sum(cm_tabla) - TP - FP - FN

# Calcular métricas
precision <- TP / (TP + FP)
recall    <- TP / (TP + FN)
f1        <- 2 * (precision * recall) / (precision + recall)
accuracy  <- sum(TP) / sum(cm_tabla)

metricas <- data.frame(
  Clase     = names(TP),
  TP, FP, FN, TN,
  Precision = round(precision, 3),
  Recall    = round(recall, 3),
  F1        = round(f1, 3)
)

print(metricas)
cat("\nAccuracy general:", round(accuracy, 3))
```
```{r}
# GRÁFICO DE FRONTERAS
grilla <- expand.grid(
  Petal.Length = seq(1, 7,   length.out = 300),
  Petal.Width  = seq(0, 2.7, length.out = 300)
)

grilla$clase <- predict(modelo_softmax, newdata = grilla)
grilla$prob_versicolor <- predict(modelo_softmax, 
                                   newdata = grilla, 
                                   type = "probs")[, "versicolor"]

ggplot() +
  # Regiones de color por clase predicha
  geom_tile(data = grilla,
            aes(x = Petal.Length, y = Petal.Width, fill = clase),
            alpha = 0.3) +
  # Isolíneas de probabilidad para versicolor
  geom_contour(data = grilla,
               aes(x = Petal.Length, y = Petal.Width, z = prob_versicolor),
               color = "gray30", linewidth = 0.4,
               breaks = c(0.15, 0.30, 0.45, 0.60, 0.75)) +
  # Puntos reales de entrenamiento
  geom_point(data = data.frame(X_train, Species = y_train),
             aes(x = Petal.Length, y = Petal.Width, 
                 color = Species, shape = Species),
             size = 2.5, alpha = 0.9) +
  scale_fill_manual(values  = c("#BFDBFE", "#BBF7D0", "#FDE68A")) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("#1D4ED8", "#15803D", "#B45309")) +
  labs(title    = "Softmax Regression — Fronteras de decisión",
       subtitle = "Iris dataset: 3 clases con 2 features",
       x = "Longitud del pétalo (cm)",
       y = "Ancho del pétalo (cm)",
       fill = "Clase predicha", color = "Clase real", shape = "Clase real") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(plot.title    = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(color = "gray50", hjust = 0.5))
```
